verbGlossary.DEMANDS: {target} creates a direct requirement for {source}, equipment, or material resource (e.g., steel structural system demands ironworkers and structural steel)
verbGlossary.SCALES: {source} proportionally increases the quantity of {target} required (e.g., gross building area scales the need for earthmoving equipment)
verbGlossary.SHIFTS TOWARD: {source} redirects resource needs toward {target} or equipment resource (e.g., modular/prefab content shifts toward crane-dependent assembly)
verbGlossary.ACTIVATES: {source} attribute triggers the need for {target} or material resource (e.g., specialty systems activates electricians and plumbers)
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: {source} certification or standard attribute makes {target} role mandatory (e.g., LEED certification requires commissioning agent)
verbGlossary.SPECIFIES: {source} environmental or code attribute determines the grade or type of {target} resource (e.g., climate zone specifies thermal insulation requirements)
verbGlossary.INTENSIFIES: {source} condition increases {target} staffing level beyond standard (e.g., occupied during construction intensifies safety professional staffing)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source} workforce attribute sets the headcount for {target} resource (e.g., peak workforce quantifies general laborer and safety professional deployment)
verbGlossary.RESTRICTS: {source} site constraint attribute limits availability or use of {target} resource (e.g., site constraints restrict hauling and material handling equipment access)
verbGlossary.STAFFS: {source} self-perform attribute determines the deployment of {target} (e.g., self-performed work staffs carpenters, concrete workers, and operators)
